channel.title: Creations on Qi Le
channel.description: Recent content in Creations on Qi Le

item.title: Dynamic Mobile Sensor Network Relay
item.description: Applied Kademlia, a Distributed Hash Table to build a peer-to-peer message network, which reduced the routing time by 75% compared to classic routing algorithms. Kept track of the dynamic position of mobile users by building a multi-thread control server and base stations, cut down the execution time by 28.78%. Handled requests and responses with gRPC. Updated and Stored the routing tables in PostgreSQL by utilizing libpqxx library.

item.title: Indoor Localization for Car Finding
item.description: Indoor positioning provides a new experience for the users, and becomes more and more important for the car-finding in the underground garages. Researchers have come up with many accurate indoor localization systems in the laboratory. However, they can hardly been deployed in the garages due to many challenges in real world. In this work, we propose a smartphone-based indoor positioning system LocateMe, which leverages radio signals and geomagnetic fields to provide fingerprinting-based localization service in garages.

item.title: Machine Learning
item.description: BOSH (Bosh Outer SHell) “…is an open source tool for release engineering, deployment, lifecycle management, and monitoring of distributed systems.” And it’s amazingly powerful. This examples uses BOSH to provision an Alassian vendor app running on JDK along with the support Postgres database and agents to support it. The releases manages the health of services and will automatically provision, start/stop processes across the various services.

item.title: Reflection removal without training: a single image perspective
item.description: Deep Image Prior(DIP), which uses a randomly initialized neural network as a handcrafted prior for standard inverse problems, such as denoising, superresolution, and inpainting, is used in this project. We add the total variation norm to enhance the performance of DIP and propose an effective algorithm to stop the iteration automatically to avoid the degradation of the image.

item.title: SynSpot
item.description: Develop Python-based software and related infrastructure for research purposes, which means the project would help researchers in utilizing the data with Machine Learning Algorithm. This application to help various entities assist each other in supervised learning tasks without sharing data, models, and objective function.

item.title: User-level Thread Library
item.description: BOSH (Bosh Outer SHell) “…is an open source tool for release engineering, deployment, lifecycle management, and monitoring of distributed systems.” And it’s amazingly powerful. This examples uses BOSH to provision an Alassian vendor app running on JDK along with the support Postgres database and agents to support it. The releases manages the health of services and will automatically provision, start/stop processes across the various services.

item.title: Web Application
item.description: Designed a Game Information Interactive Reviewing Website to interact with visitors. Increasing cost time for associative-word search from 3951ms to 6ms under 1000 executions using implemented Trie Tree and Python Session, compared to PostgreSQL fuzzy search. Supported the authentication using flask + authlib + auth0. Built a data warehouse to store multi-sourced data about game Information and updated comments with PostgreSQL. Utilized jQuery and Pure CSS to build the frontend which provides users with self-guide exploration for game content
